headline: Babylon Protocol: Bitcoin-native restaking on Cosmos SDK; zero traditional contract addresses discoverable; governance and control via Babylon Genesis chain and covenant committee
short_headline: Bitcoin-native protocol; governance via Babylon Genesis chain

findings.text: Babylon is a Bitcoin-native protocol (not EVM-based); no traditional contract address name-tags found on Bitcoin block explorers. Bitcoin staking occurs natively via Bitcoin Script transactions, not smart contracts.

Official deployments doc does not list contract addresses; protocol operates via Bitcoin Script staking on Bitcoin mainnet and Babylon Genesis (Cosmos SDK-based L1) coordination layer. No canonical deployed contracts page identified.
No audit links recorded in protocol.audit_links; website mentions audits (Coinspect, Zellic, Cantina Security) but PDFs not fetched in this run.

GitHub repo at https://github.com/babylonlabs-io/babylon is primarily Go/Cosmos SDK codebase; no hardhat-deploy or broadcast artifacts discoverable. Repository structure suggests Cosmos chain implementation.
Protocol.chains=[Bitcoin]; single chain. Babylon Genesis is infrastructure layer, not a user-facing deployment target. Bitcoin mainnet is sole user-interaction surface.

No factory pattern identified; Bitcoin staking is transaction-native, not contract-enumerable. Finality providers and validators operate on Babylon Genesis chain via state transitions, not child-contract instantiation.

Critical admin actors identified: Covenant Committee (consensus-requiring signature quorum for unbonding/slashing), Babylon validators (run Babylon Genesis), Finality Providers (Bitcoin public keys for delegation). Role taxonomy adapted for non-EVM context.
All addresses and control roles sourced from fetched docs, GitHub, and website; no snippet-only sightings in admin_addresses[].

rationale.verdict: Discovery phase incomplete due to protocol architecture mismatch. Babylon is a Bitcoin-native, non-EVM protocol. Traditional 'contract addresses' do not apply. Control flows through Babylon Genesis chain validators, covenant committee multisig, and Bitcoin transaction scripting. Future slices must enumerate Babylon Genesis validator addresses, covenant committee signer addresses, and known finality provider public keys. This run identified repositories and off-chain governance channels but found zero on-chain EVM-like contract addresses to catalog.

evidence.shows: Official protocol website; lists GitHub, docs, bug bounty, forum, and mentions audits (Coinspect, Zellic, Cantina); confirms Bitcoin staking and Babylon Genesis chain as core infrastructure.
Official documentation site; describes Babylon Genesis as Cosmos SDK-based coordination layer; Bitcoin staking via native Bitcoin Script; no EVM or traditional contracts.
Primary source repository; Go/Cosmos SDK codebase; implements Babylon node, BTC staking module, finality module; no Solidity or EVM-based contracts.
Network configuration repository; bbn-1 is mainnet; documents Babylon validator setup, finality provider registration, covenant committee; Bitcoin staking phase documentation.

unknowns: D1-Bitcoin-explorer: Bitcoin block explorer name-tag search not performed; Bitcoin mainnet staking contract addresses (if identifiable via explorer) not discovered.
D2-deployed-contracts-doc: No canonical 'Deployed Contracts' page found; Babylon Genesis validators, covenant committee address, and finality provider key registry exist but not enumerated this run.
D3-audit-PDFs: Audit links (Coinspect, Zellic, Cantina Security) mentioned on website but PDFs not fetched; scope sections not extracted.
D4-GitHub-deployments: Babylon Genesis validator keys and covenant committee multisig addresses not extracted from networks repo or chain state.
D6-factory-enumeration-offchain: No factory pattern applicable; however, Babylon Genesis active validator set and finality provider registry are on-chain enumerables not yet explored.
D8-covenant-committee-addresses: Covenant committee signer addresses (critical control layer) not discovered; requires either on-chain state read (Babylon Genesis) or off-chain governance docs.

protocol_metadata.about: Babylon Protocol enables Bitcoin holders to stake BTC natively on the Bitcoin blockchain to provide economic security to Proof-of-Stake networks and other decentralized systems. Bitcoin staking operates via Bitcoin Script-based transactions (trustless and self-custodial), coordinated by the Babylon Genesis chain (a Cosmos SDK-based Layer 1). The protocol introduces Trustless Bitcoin Vaults that use BitVM3 to allow BTC to serve as collateral in DeFi applications, eliminating the need for centralized bridges.
